selection_rule: reject any task or protocol failure
break close ties conservatively using fewer tool calls and the less restrictive setting

parent_instruction_compaction.reason: The user stopped the initial release batch before completion and requested the parent skill use the same terse machine-oriented form as the worker contract.
parent_instruction_compaction.change: The parent execution contract is now thirteen key-value lines, the compaction handoff is five key-value lines, and the auto-Spark availability preflight is five key-value lines. Configuration knob winners are unchanged.

release_hardening.reason: The first development Spark gate completed correctly but omitted the required parent_work_replaced literal prefix.
release_hardening.change: Made the two-line Spark brief template an explicit pre-spawn check; no native tuning knob changed.

availability_hardening.reason: The replacement development gate completed locally after incorrectly treating the capability-validated spark_worker role as unavailable.
availability_hardening.change: The Spark arm now tells the parent that capability preflight succeeded and permits local fallback only after an explicit spawn-tool error.

worker_budget_hardening.reason: The third development gate routed correctly but an uncapped worker expanded to 40 tool calls and emitted one non-RTK shell command.
worker_budget_hardening.change: Bounded Spark workers now batch assigned reads, stop at twelve tool calls, never shell tool names, and return an evidence capsule of at most 250 words.

rtk_probe_and_prompt_hardening.reason: The fourth development gate used an invalid rtk -c probe, then treated the wrapped command error as a missing RTK executable; its parent prompt also embedded one marker mid-sentence.
rtk_probe_and_prompt_hardening.change: Workers now start with a real required RTK command and distinguish wrapped-command failure from executable launch failure. Every required case and the Spark arm require the two delegation markers as standalone opening lines.

spark_contract_simplification.reason: Four excluded gates showed that repeated prompt-format and RTK-probe rules delayed first spawn by 28.390 to 68.108 seconds and optimized compliance instead of work replacement.
spark_contract_simplification.change: Spark now spawns as the first tool action for required named partitions. The parent supplies only partition IDs, exclusive paths, task, and result contract; the worker has no artificial tool-call cap. Claimed parent_work_replaced text was removed, and the harness proves replacement from completed useful child work plus absence of parent read/write overlap.
spark_contract_simplification.worker_ranges: required cases use min 1 and no maximum; observed count and savings per worker are metrics
spark_contract_simplification.selection_objective: correctness and protocol first; parent tokens primary; wall-time reduction fallback; child and combined tokens disclosed

limitations: Each setting was observed once, so the study selects a candidate rather than estimating variance.
The two knobs were screened sequentially on different development cases; the exact combined candidate is gated on the confirmation workload before the release matrix.
Latency was contention-affected and is not publishable.
